mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates raise the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day S&P 500 returns follow elevated FEDFUNDS levels in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in nonfarm payrolls and industrial production indicates simultaneous labor market strength and output expansion, which boosts aggregate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 quarterly returns.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in PAYEMS and INDPRO coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 40% of instances over a rolling 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales decline for two consecutive months, the contraction in consumer demand reduces corporate revenue visibility, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines 2 months in a row
failure_condition: Two consecutive monthly declines in RSAFS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered events within a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, concurrent increases in the unemployment rate confirm restrictive policy is cooling the labor market, reducing earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, month-over-month increases in UNRATE fail to predict negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with directional accuracy above 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong residential investment and construction employment, which signals broad economic momentum and drives positive S&P 500 quarterly returns through multiplier effects.
failure_condition: Monthly increases in HOUST are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, leading to positive earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 weekly returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of monthly signal observations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price index growth increases discount rates via expected monetary tightening and reduces real corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 monthly returns.
failure_condition: Month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.5% are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered months over a rolling 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth signals aggregate demand expansion that lifts corporate revenues across sectors, generating positive S&P 500 quarterly forward returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly increases in GDPC1 are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45% of observations over a 10-year rolling window.
